2. Example A-2

[00257] 2-Amino-6-methoxybenzothiazole was reacted with hot aqueous HBr at a temperature of >70 °C for about 3 hours and then the clear solution was cooled to ambient temperature overnight. The precipitated solids were collected, dissolved in hot water and the pH was adjusted to between 4.5-5.5. The resultant solids were collected, dried and re-crystallized from isopropanol. Second crop material was collected. The solids were vacuum dried to give 2-amino-6-hydroxybenzothiazole.

[00258] The reaction progress was monitored by thin layer chromatography (TLC). The product was isolated as a white solid, with 99.4% purity (HPLC area %). JH NMR (300 MHz, DMSO-if6) was collected, which conformed to structure.

3. Example A-3

[00259] A 22-L 3-neck round bottom flask was equipped with a mechanical agitator, thermocouple probe, a reflux condenser, and a heating mantle. The flask was charged with hydrobromic acid (14 L, 123.16 mol, 13.10 equiv). Heating was initiated and 2- amino-6-methoxybenzothiazole was added (1.7 kg, 9.4 mol, 1.00 equiv) over 10 minutes with stirring. The heating of the reaction mixture was continued to reflux, and maintained (>107 °C) for approximately 5 hours. The reaction mixture turned into a clear solution between 75 °C and 85 °C. The reaction progress was monitored by TLC until no starting material was observed (A -0.5 mL reaction mixture aliquot was diluted with -0.5 mL water as a clear solution, neutralized with sodium acetate to pH -5 and extracted with 1 mL dichloromethane. The organic layer was spotted: 5%

methanol/dichloromethane; Rf (product) = 0.35; Rf (starting material) = 0.40).

[00260] The reaction mixture was cooled to - 20 °C (overnight). White solids precipitated. The solids were filtered on a polypropylene filter and pressed to remove as much hydrobromic acid from the solids as possible to facilitate the subsequent pH adjustment step. The slightly wet crude product was dissolved in hot (50 °C) water (5 L). The clear solution was filtered to remove any insoluble material present, and the solids were washed with 50 °C water. The filtrate was cooled to 10 °C. The cooled filtrate was neutralized with sodium acetate (- 1.0 kg) to pH 4.5 to 5.5 with vigorous stirring. A thick white solid precipitated. The solids were collected by filtration, and washed with cool (-10 °C) water (2 x 1.0 L) and air dried.

[00261] The wet crude product was slurried in hot (50 °C) isopropanol (3 L) briefly and allowed to stand in a cool room (-5 °C) overnight. The solids were collected by filtration and washed with methyl ferf-butylether (2 x 500 mL). The solids were dried in a vacuum oven overnight (<30 mm Hg) at 30 °C (first crop). Yield: 1068 g (68%), white solid. HPLC: 99.4% (area). JH NMR (300 MHz, DMSO- ) conformed to structure.

[00262] The organic filtrate was collected in a total volume of 1.0 L, cooled to 10 °C. The off-white solids were precipitated and collected by filtration. The solids were dried in a vacuum oven overnight (<30 mm Hg) at 30 °C (second crop). Yield: 497 g (32%), off-white solid. HPLC: 99.8% (area).

[00263] The overall yield combining the first crop and the second crop was 1565 g, (99%).

B. Preparation of 2-(4-nitrophenyl)imidazo[2,l-b]benzothiazol-7-ol

1. Example B-l

[00264] A 3-L 3-neck round bottom flask fitted with a condenser, a heating mantle, and a mechanical stirrer was charged with H-butanol (1.5 L), followed by 2-amino-6- hydroxybenzothiazole (75 g, 0.45 mol, 1.0 equiv), 2-bromo-4'-nitroacetophenone (121 g, 0.50 mol, 1.1 equiv), and sodium bicarbonate (41.6 g, 0.50 mol, 1.0 equiv). The reaction temperature was gradually raised to reflux and maintained at reflux (110-115 °C) for 2-3 hr. During the temperature increase, the reaction mass turned into a clear solution and then immediately changed into an orange colored suspension at 65-75 °C. The progress of the reaction was monitored by HPLC analysis every 1 hr (reaction mass sample was submitted to QC). When the level of 2-amino-6-hydroxybenzothiazole was less than 2%, the reaction was substantially complete.

[00265] The reaction mass was slowly cooled to 50-60 °C and then further cooled to 0-5 °C and stirred for 15 min. The precipitated solids were collected by filtration, and dried on the filter. The wet cake was transferred in to a 1-L round bottom flask, and water (600 mL) was added. The suspension was stirred for 0.5 hr and filtered, and the solid was dried on the filter. The wet cake was again taken in to a 1-L round bottom flask and stirred with acetone (200 mL). The slurry was filtered and washed with acetone (2 X 100 mL), and the solid was dried on the filter, unloaded and further dried in a vacuum oven at ambient temperature to give the product 2-(4-nitrophenyl)imidazo[2,l- b]benzothiazol-7-ol (V) (120 g, 85.7% yield, HPLC purity = 98.7%). JH NMR (DMSO- d6, 500 MHz): δ 9.96 (s, 1H, OH), 8.93 (s, 1H), 8.27 (d, 2H), 8.06 (d, 2H), 7.78 (d, 1H), 7.38 (d, 1H), 6.97 (dd, 1H); MS (m/z) = 312 [M+ + 1].

The flask was charged with 2-amino-6-hydroxybenzothiazole (1068 g, 6.43 mol, 1.0 equiv) and ethanol (200 proof, 32.0 L), and the suspension was stirred for 10 minutes. 2-Bromo-4- nitroacetophenone (1667 g, 6.49 mol, 1.01 equiv) was added in one portion. The reaction mixture was heated to reflux (78 °C). The reflux was maintained for approximately 25 hours, resulting in a yellow suspension. C. Preparation of 7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)-2-(4- nitrophenyl)imidazo[2, 1 -bjbenzothiazole

1. Example C-l

[00273] To a 2000-L glass-lined (GL) reactor was charged DMF (298 kg), and the agitator was started. Under a nitrogen blanket, the reactor was charged with 2-(4- nitrophenyl)imidazo[2,l-&]benzothiazol-7-ol (36.8 kg, 118.2 mol, 1.0 equiv), 4-(2- chloroethyl)morpholine hydrochloride (57.2-66.0 kg, 307.3-354.6 mol, 2.6-3.0 equiv), tetrabutylammonium iodide (8.7 kg, 23.6 mol, 0.2 equiv) and potassium carbonate (49.0 kg, 354.6 mol, 3.0 equiv). The resulting yellow suspension was heated and stirred at 90 + 5 °C for at least 15 minutes, then the temperature was increased to 110 + 5 °C. The mixture was stirred for at least 1 hour and then sampled. The reaction was deemed complete if 2-(4-nitrophenyl) imidazo[2,l-&]benzothiazol-7-ol was <1% by HPLC. If the reaction was not complete, the heating was continued and the reaction sampled. If the reaction was incomplete after 6 hours, additional 4-(2-chloroethyl)morpholine hydrochloride may be charged. In general, additional charges of 4-(2- chloroethyl)morpholine hydrochloride had not been necessary at the given scale.

[00274] The reactor was cooled to 20 + 5 °C and charged with water (247 kg) and acetone (492 kg). The mixture was agitated at 20 + 5 °C for at least 1 hour. The product (VII) was isolated by filtration or centrifuge, and washed with water and acetone, and then dried in a vacuum oven at 45 °C to constant weight to give a yellow solid (46.2 kg, 92% yield, HPLC purity = 97.4% by area). JH NMR (300 MHz, DMSO- ) conformed to structure.

2. Example C-2

[00275] 2-(4-Nitrophenyl)imidazo[2, l-b]benzothiazol-7-ol, 4-(2-chloroethyl)- morpholine hydrochloride, potassium carbonate, and tetrabutylammonium iodide were added to N,N-dimethylformamide forming a yellow suspension that was heated at a temperature of >50 °C for over 3 hours. The reaction was cooled and the solids were collected, slurried into water, filtered, slurried into acetone, filtered and washed with acetone to give yellow solids that were dried under vacuum to give 7-(2-morpholin-4-yl- ethoxy)-2-(4-nitrophenyl)imidazo[2,l-b]benzothiazole.

[00276] The reaction progress was monitored by thin layer chromatography (TLC). The product was isolated as a yellow solid, with 99% purity (HPLC area %), and a water content of 0.20%. Infrared (IR) spectrum was collected, which conformed to structure.

3. Example C-3

[00277] A 50-L 3-neck round bottom flask was equipped with a mechanical agitator, a thermocouple probe, a drying tube, a reflux condenser, and a heating mantle. The flask was charged with 2-(4-nitrophenyl)imidazo [2,l-&]benzothiazol-7-ol (1.770 kg, 5.69 mol, 1.0 equiv), N,N-dimethylformamide (18.0 L), 4-(2-chloroethyl)morpholine hydrochloride (2.751 kg, 14.78 mol, 2.6 equiv), potassium carbonate (2.360 kg, 17.10 mol, 3.0 equiv), and tetrabutylammonium iodide (0.130 kg, 0.36 mol, 0.06 equiv) with stirring. The resulting yellow suspension was heated to about 90 °C to 95 °C, maintaining the temperature for approximately 5 hours. The reaction was monitored by TLC until no starting material was observed (20% methanol / ethyl acetate; Rf (product) = 0.15; Rf (starting material) = 0.85).

[00278] The reaction mixture was cooled to -10 °C, and the yellow solids were collected by filtration on a polypropylene filter pad. The solids were slurried in water (2 X 5 L) and filtered. The crude wet product was slurried in acetone (5 L), filtered, and the solids were rinsed with acetone (2 X 1.5 L). The solids were dried in a vacuum oven (<10 mm Hg) at 45 °C. Yield: 2.300 kg (95%), yellow solid. TLC: R/ = 0.15 (20% methanol / EtOAc). HPLC: 95.7% (area). JH NMR (300 MHz, DMSO-i¾) conformed to the structure.

4. Example C-4

[00280] To a reactor were added 2-(4-nitrophenyl)imidazo [2,l-&]benzothiazol-7-ol (1.0 kg), 4-(2-chloroethyl)morpholine hydrochloride (1.6 kg), tetrabutylammonium iodide (0.24 kg), and potassium carbonate (1.3 kg, anhydrous, extra fine, hydroscopic). N,N-Dimethylformamide (DMF) (8.6 L) was added into the reactor. The DMF used had water content of no more than 0.05% w/w. The mixture was stirred for between 15 and 30 minutes to render a homogeneous suspension, which was heated to between 85 °C and 95 °C and stirred at between 85 °C and 95 °C for 15 to 30 minutes. The mixture was then heated to between 105 °C and 120 °C and stirred at between 105 °C and 120 °C (e.g. , 115 °C) until the reaction was complete (as determined by HPLC to monitor the consumption of starting material). In some embodiments, if necessary (e.g. , if after 6 hours the reaction was not complete as indicated by HPLC analysis), additional 4-(2- chloroethyl)morpholine hydrochloride (0.03 kg) may be added and the reaction mixture stirred at between 105 °C and 120 °C (e.g. , 115 °C) until reaction completion.

[00281] The reaction mixture was cooled to between 20 °C and 30 °C (e.g. , over a period of 3 hours). To another reactor was added deionized water (7.6 L) and acetone (15 L). The mixture of water and acetone was then added into the reaction mixture while maintaining the temperature at between 20 °C and 30 °C. The mixture was then stirred for 1 to 2 hours at a temperature of between 20 °C and 30 °C. The mixture was filtered, and the solid was washed with deionized water (e.g. , about 45x deionized water) until pH of washes was below 8. The solid was then washed with acetone (9.7 L). The solid was dried under vacuum at a temperature of less than 50 °C until the water content by Karl-Fischer was less than 0.30% w/w and TGA curve showed a mass loss of less than 0.30% w/w at about 229 °C (sampling approximately every 6 hours). The desired product was obtained in about 89% yield having about 99% purity by HPLC.

D. Preparation of 7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)-2-(4- aminophenyl)imidazo [2, 1 -bjbenzothiazole

[00286] To a 200-L high pressure (HP) reactor was charged a slurry of 7-(2- morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)-2-(4-nitrophenyl)imidazo [2,l-&]benzothiazole (VII) (7.50 kg, 17.7 mol, 1.0 equiv) in methanol (30 kg). The container was rinsed with additional methanol (10 kg) and the rinse was charged to the reactor. The reactor was then charged with THF (67 kg) and methanol (19 kg). The contents were agitated and the reactor was flushed with nitrogen by alternating nitrogen and vacuum. Vacuum was applied to the reactor and Raney Ni catalyst (1.65 kg, 0.18 wt. equiv) was charged through a sample line. Water (1 kg) was charged through the sample line to rinse the line. The reactor was flushed with nitrogen by alternating nitrogen and vacuum. The reactor was then vented and heated to 50 °C. The reactor was closed and pressurized with hydrogen gas to 15 psi keeping the internal temperature below 55 °C. The reactor was vented and re- pressurized a total of 5 times, then pressurized to 150 psi with hydrogen gas. The contents were agitated at 50 °C for at least 4 hours. At this point a hydrogen uptake test was applied: The reactor was re-pressurized to 150 psi and checked after 1 hour. If a pressure drop of more than 5 psi was observed, the process was repeated. Once the pressure drop remained < 5 psi, the reactor was vented and sampled. The reaction was deemed complete when 7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)-2-(4-nitrophenyl)imidazo [2,1- 6]benzothiazole (VII) was < 0.5% by HPLC.

[00287] The reactor was flushed with nitrogen as shown above. The 200-L HP reactor was connected to the 2000-L GL reactor passing through a bag filter and polish filter. The bag filter and polish filter were heated with steam. The 200-L HP reactor was pressurized (3 psi nitrogen) and its contents were filtered into the 2000-L reactor. The filtrates were hot. The 200-L reactor was vented and charged with THF (67 kg) and methanol (59 kg), the reactor agitated, and filtered into the 2000-L GL reactor.

[00288] A total of 6 reductions (46.2 kg processed) were carried out and the combined batches were concentrated by vacuum distillation (without exceeding an internal temperature of 40 °C) to a volume of -180 L. The reactor was cooled to 20 °C and charged with heptane (250 kg) and again vacuum distilled to a volume of -180 L. The reactor was charged with heptane (314 kg) and agitated at 20 °C for at least 1 hour, and then the product was isolated by centrifugation or collection on a Nutsche filter, washing with heptanes (2-5 kg per portion for centrifugation, followed by a 10-20 kg heptanes rinse of the reactor; or 94 kg for Nutsche filtration, rinsing the reactor first). The cake was blown dry, transferred to a vacuum oven and dried to constant weight maintaining a temperature < 50 °C to give the desired product (VIII) (34.45 kg, 80% yield, HPLC purity = 97.9%).

Raney nickel was added and the vessel was pressurized with hydrogen and stirred for >24 hours. The reaction mixture was concentrated to a thick paste and diluted with methyl ferf-butyl ether. The resulting solids were filtered and washed with methyl ferf-butyl ether and dried under vacuum to give 7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)-2-(4-aminophenyl) imidazo [2, 1 -bjbenzothiazole.

[00290] The reaction progress was monitored by thin layer chromatography (TLC). The product was isolated as a yellow solid, with 99% purity (HPLC area %). IR was collected, which conformed to structure.

3. Example D-3

[00291] Into a 5-gallon autoclave, 7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)-2-(4-nitrophenyl) imidazo[2,l-&]benzothiazole (580 g, 1.37 mol, 1.0 equiv), THF (7.5 L), methanol (7.5 L, AR) and -55 g of decanted Raney nickel catalyst were added. The reaction vessel was purged with nitrogen (3 X 50 psi) and hydrogen (2 X 50 psi), with stirring briefly under pressure and then venting. The autoclave was pressurized with hydrogen (150 psi). The mixture was stirred and the hydrogen pressure was maintained at 150 psi for over 24 hours with repressurization as necessary. The reaction progress was monitored by TLC (10% methanol / chloroform with 1 drop ammonium hydroxide; Rf (product) 0.20; Rf (SM) 0.80). The reaction was substantially complete when the TLC indicated no starting material present, typically after 24 hours of stirring at 150 psi. The hydrogenation was continued at 150 psi for a minimum of 4 hours or until completion if starting material was still present after the initial 4 hours.

[00292] The reaction mixture was filtered through a Buchner funnel equipped with a glass fiber filter topped with a paper filter. Unreacted starting material was removed together with the catalyst. The filtrate was concentrated to a total volume of 0.5 L, and the residue was triturated with methyl ferf-butyl ether (0.5 L). The resultant solids were collected by filtration, and washed with methyl ferf-butyl ether (0.3 L) (first crop).

[00293] The filtrate was concentrated to dryness and the residue was diluted with methyl ferf-butyl ether (2 L). The resultant solids were collected by filtration, washing with methyl ferf-butyl ether (0.5 L) (second crop).

[00294] The solids were dried in a vacuum oven (<10 mm Hg) at 25 °C. Yield: 510 g (95%), beige solid. TLC: R/ 0.2 (10% methanol / chloroform with one drop of ammonium hydroxide). HPLC: 99.0% (area). JH NMR (300 MHz, DMSO-i¾) conformed to the structure.

5. Example D-5

[00297] To a pressure reactor under nitrogen atmosphere was added a slurry of Raney Nickel in water (0.22 kg) (e.g. about 0.14 kg dry catalyst in water) and the charging line was rinsed with deionized water (0.13 L). To another reactor (Reactor B) were added methanol (5.05 L) and 7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)-2-(4-nitrophenyl)imidazo [2, 1- &]benzothiazole (1.0 kg), and the mixture was stirred for between 15 and 30 minutes to render a homogenous suspension. The suspension was transferred to the pressure reactor. Reactor B was washed with methanol (4.88 L) and the wash was transferred to the pressure reactor. Tetrahydrofuran (10.1 L) was added to the pressure reactor.

Hydrogen was charged to the pressure reactor to a pressure of between 2.0 bar and 3.0 bar. The reactor was heated to a temperature of between 45 °C and 55 °C. Hydrogen was then charged to the pressure reactor to a pressure of between 6.0 bar and 7.0 bar. The mixture was stirred at a temperature of between 45 °C and 55 °C (e.g. , 50 °C), while maintaining the hydrogen pressure between 6.0 bar and 7.0 bar until reaction completion (as determined by HPLC to monitor the consumption of starting material).

[00298] The mixture was filtered while maintaining the temperature at between 35 °C and 50 °C. The pressure reactor and the filter were washed with a mixture of THF (10.1 L) and methanol (9.93 L) preheated to a temperature of between 45 °C and 55 °C (e.g. , 50 °C). The combined filtrate was concentrated to a volume of between 2.4 L and 2.8 L under vacuum at a temperature of no more than 40 °C, and a precipitate was formed. Methanol (7.5 L) was added. The slurry was cooled to a temperature of between 5 °C and -5 °C (e.g. , over 3 hours) and stirred for at least 1 hour (e.g. , for 3 hours) while maintaining the temperature at between 5 °C and -5 °C. The suspension was filtered. The solid was washed with methanol (2 X 1.2 L). The solid was then dried under vacuum at a temperature of less than 50 °C until the methanol and THF contents were each less than 3000 ppm as analyzed by GC (e.g. , less than 1500 ppm). The desired product was obtained in about 88.5% yield having about 99% purity by HPLC.

E. Preparation of phenyl 5-£er£-butylisoxazol-3-ylcarbamate

[00299] The jacket temperature of a 200-L glass-lined (GL) reactor was set to 20 °C. To the reactor was charged 5-ieri-butylisoxazole-3-amine (15.0 kg, 107.0 mol, 1.0 equiv), then K2C03 (19.5 kg, 141.2 mol, 1.3 equiv) and anhydrous THF (62 kg).

Agitation was started and then phenyl chloroformate (17.6 kg, 112.4 mol, 1.05 equiv) was charged. The charging line was rinsed with additional anhydrous THF (5 kg). The reaction was agitated at 20 + 5 °C for at least 3 hours then sampled. The reaction was deemed complete if 5-£er£-butylisoxazole-3-amine was < 2% by HPLC. If the reaction was not complete after 6 hours, additional K2CO3 and phenyl chloroformate may be added to drive the reaction to completion.

[00300] Once complete, the reaction was filtered (Nutsche). The filter was rinsed with THF (80 kg). The filtrate was vacuum distilled without exceeding an internal temperature of 40 °C until -50 L remained. Water (188 kg) and ethanol (45 L) were charged, and the mixture was agitated for at least 3 hours with a jacket temperature of 20 °C. The resulting solid was isolated by centrifugation or collection on a Nutsche filter, rinsed with water (2-5 kg for each centrifugation portion; 30 kg for Nutsche filtration) and blow-dried. The solid was then dried to constant weight in a vacuum oven (45 °C) to give the desired product (19.4 kg, 92% yield, HPLC purity = 97.4%). On an 800 g scale, 1559 g of the desired product (98% yield) was obtained with a 99.9% HPLC purity. JH NMR (DMSO-i¾) δ 11.17 (s, 1H); 7.4 (m, 2H); 7.2 (m, 3H); 1.2 (s, 9H). LCMS (M+H)+ 261.

F. Preparation of N-(5-ieri-butyl-isoxazol-3-yl)-N'-{4-[7-(2-morpholin-4-yl- ethoxy)imidazo[2, 1 -b] [ 1 ,3 ]benzothiazol-2-yl]phenyl } urea

1. Example F-l

[00301] The jacket of a 2000-L GL reactor was set to 20 °C and the reactor was charged with 7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)-2-(4-aminophenyl)imidazo[2,l- &]benzothiazole (26.7 kg, 67.8 mol, 1.0 equiv), 3-amino-5-?-butylisoxazole phenyl carbamate (19.4 kg, 74.5 mol, 1.1 equiv), DMAP (0.5 kg, 4.4 mol, 0.06 equiv), and DCM (anhydrous, 260 kg). Agitation was started, triethylamine (1.0 kg, 10.2 mol, 0.15 equiv) was charged followed by additional DCM (5 kg) through the charging line. The reaction was heated to reflux (-40 °C) and agitated for at least 20 hours with complete dissolution observed followed by product crystallizing from solution after -30 minutes. The reaction was sampled and deemed complete when HPLC analysis showed a ratio of compound (VIII) to compound (I) < 1%.

[00302] The reactor was cooled to 0 °C and stirred for at least 2 hours. The content of the reactor were isolated by centrifuge. Each portion was rinsed with 2-3 kg of cold (0 °C) DCM and spun dry for at least 5 minutes with a 10 psi nitrogen purge. For the final portion, the reactor was rinsed with 10 kg of cold (0 °C) DCM and transferred to the centrifuge where it was spun dry for at least 5 minutes with a 10 psi nitrogen purge. The combined filter cakes were transferred to a vacuum tray dryer and dried to constant weight at 50 °C and at least >20 inches of Hg to give the desired product (I) (35.05 kg, 92% yield, HPLC purity = 98.8%). Phenol was the major impurity detected (0.99%); and three other impurities (<0.10%) were detected. JH NMR (300 MHz, DMSO- ) conformed to structure.

4. Example F-4

[00305] To Reactor A under a nitrogen atmosphere was added 7-(2-morpholin-4-yl- ethoxy)-2-(4-aminophenyl)imidazo[2,l-&]benzothiazole (1 kg) and DMAP (0.02 kg). To Reactor B under a nitrogen atmosphere was added 3-amino-5-?-butylisoxazole phenyl carbamate (0.73 kg) and DCM (5.6 L). The DCM used had a water content of less than 0.05 % w/w. The mixture in Reactor B was stirred until dissolution. The solution was transferred into Reactor A (the solution can be filtered into Reactor A to remove any insoluble impurities in the carbamate starting material), and the mixture was stirred in Reactor A. Reactor B was washed with DCM (0.8 L) and the wash was transferred into Reactor A. Reactor A was washed with DCM (0.9 L). To Reactor A was added triethylamine (0.1 L) and the charging vessel and lines were rinsed with DCM (0.1 L) into Reactor A. The mixture was then heated to reflux and stirred at reflux until reaction completion (as determined by HPLC to monitor the consumption of starting material).

[00306] The reaction mixture was cooled (e.g. , over 2 to 3 hours) to a temperature of between -5 °C and 5 °C (e.g. , 0 °C). The mixture was then stirred for 2 to 3 hours at a temperature of between -5 °C and 5 °C (e.g. , 0 °C). The suspension was filtered. The solid was washed with cool DCM (2 X 1.5 L) (pre-cooled to a temperature of between -5 °C and 5 °C). The solid was dried under vacuum at a temperature of less than 45 °C until the DCM content was less than 1000 ppm (e.g., below 600 ppm) as analyzed by GC. The desired product was obtained having about 99% purity by HPLC.

G. Preparation of N-(5-ieri-butyl-isoxazol-3-yl)-N'-{4-[7-(2-morpholin-4-yl- ethoxy)imidazo[2, l-b] [1 ,3]benzothiazol-2-yl]phenyl }urea dihydrochloride

1. Example G-l

[00307] The jacket of a 2000-L GL reactor was set to 20 °C and the reactor was charged with N-(5-ieri-butyl-isoxazol-3-yl)-N'-{4-[7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)imidazo [2, 1-&][1, 3]benzothiazol-2-yl]phenyl}urea (35.0 kg, 62.4 mol, 1.0 equiv) followed by methanol (553 kg). Agitation was started and the reaction mixture was heated to reflux (-65 °C). Concentrated aqueous HC1 (15.4 kg, 156.0 mol, 2.5 equiv) was charged rapidly (<5 minutes) and the charge line was rinsed into the reactor with methanol (12 kg). Addition of less than 2.0 equivalents of HC1 normally resulted in the formation of an insoluble solid. The reaction mixture was heated at reflux for at least 1 hour. Upon HC1 addition, the slurry dissolved and almost immediately the salt started to crystallize, leaving insufficient time for a polish filtration.

[00308] The reactor was cooled to 20 °C and the product was isolated by filtration (Nutsche) rinsing the reactor and then the cake with methanol (58 kg). The solid was then dried in a vacuum oven (50 °C) to constant weight to give the desired

dihydrochloride salt (35 kg, 89% yield, HPLC purity = 99.94%). JH NMR (300 MHz, DMSO-i¾) conformed to structure.

2. Example G-2

[00309] Concentrated HC1 was added to a suspension of N-(5-ieri-butyl-isoxazol-3- yl)-N'-{4-[7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)imidazo[2,l-&][l,3]benzothiazol-2- yl]phenyl}urea in warm methanol forming a solution that slowly began to precipitate. The reaction mixture was refluxed for over 2 hours and then stirred overnight at ambient temperature. The dihydrochloride salt was collected and dried under vacuum.

3. Example G-3

[00310] A 50-L 3-neck round bottom flask was equipped with a mechanical agitator, a thermocouple probe, a nitrogen inlet, a drying tube, a reflux condenser, an addition funnel, and a heating mantle. The flask was charged with N-(5-ieri-butyl-isoxazol-3-yl)- N'-{4-[7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)imidazo[2,l-&][l,3]benzothiazol-2-yl]phenyl}urea (775 g, 1.38 mol, 1.0 equiv) and MeOH (40 L, AR). The resulting off-white suspension was heated to reflux (68 °C). A clear solution did not form. HC1 (37% aqueous) (228 mL, 3.46 mol, 2.5 equiv) was added over 5 minutes at 68 °C. The reaction mixture turned into a clear solution and then a new precipitate formed within approximately 3 minutes. Heating was continued at reflux for approximately 5 hours. The reaction mixture was allowed to cool to ambient temperature overnight. The off-white solids were collected by filtration on a polypropylene filter, washing with MeOH (2 X 1 L, AR). [00311] Two lots of material prepared in this manner were combined (740 g and 820 g). The combined solids were slurried in methanol (30 L) over 30 minutes at reflux and allowed to cool to the room temperature. The solids were collected by filtration on a polypropylene filter, rinsing with methanol (2 X 1.5 L). The solids were dried in a vacuum oven (<10 mm Hg) at 40 °C. Yield: 1598 g (84%), off-white solid. HPLC: 98.2% (area). MS: 561.2 (M+l)+. JH NMR (300 MHz, DMSO-i¾) conformed to the structure. Elemental Analysis (EA): Theory, 54.97 %C; 5.41 %H; 13.26 %N; 5.06 %S; 11.19 %C1; Actual, 54.45 %C; 5.46 %H; 13.09 %N; 4.99 %S; 10.91 %C1.

4. Example G-4

[00312] Into a 50-L 3-neck round bottom flask equipped with a mechanical stirrer, a heating mantle, a condenser and a nitrogen inlet, were charged N-(5-ieri-butyl-isoxazol- 3-yl)-N'-{4-[7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)imidazo[2,l-&][l,3]benzothiazol-2- yl]phenyl}urea (1052.4 g, 1.877 mol, 1.0 equiv) and methanol (21 L). The reactor was heated and stirred. At an internal temperature of > 50 °C, cone. HC1 (398.63 mL, 4.693 mol, 2.5 equiv) was charged over 5 minutes through an addition funnel. During the addition, the mixture changed from a pale yellow suspension to a white suspension. The internal temperature was 55 °C at the conclusion of the addition. The mixture was heated to reflux for 1 hour, then heating was discontinued and the mixture was allowed to cool to room temperature. The mixture was filtered in two portions, and each filter cake was washed with methanol (2 X 1 L), transferred to trays and dried in a vacuum oven (45 °C) to constant weight. The dried trays were combined to produce 1141.9 g of the salt (96% yield, 99.1 % HPLC purity, 10.9% chloride by titration).

[00319] HPLC analytical methods used for Examples C-l, D-l, D-4, E, F-l, F-2, F-3, G-l, and G-5 are summarized below.

[00320] Samples were diluted in 1:1 acetonitrile/water, methanol, dimethylsulfoxide, 1:1 tetrahydrofuran/water, 0.1% phosphoric acid in 75:25 water/acetonitrile, or 0.1% phosphoric acid in 4:1 water/dimethylsulfoxide. The resulting solution were run on a reverse phase HPLC with UV detection performed at 230 nm. The column temperature was 40°C. The injection volume was 5 μΐ^. The flow rate was 1.0 mL/min. The acquisition time was 25 min plus 5 min post run. The gradient was as follows:

Plus 5.0 minute re-equilibration time

[00321] Agilent 1100/1200 HPLC system or equivalent was used. A Waters XTerra RP18, 4.6 x 150 mm, 5 μιη column was used. Mobile phase A was 0.1% phosphoric acid in water. Mobile phase B was 0.1% phosphoric acid in acetonitrile. For isolated solids, about 25-30 mg sample being analyzed was accurately weighed and dissolved in the diluent, and analyzed by HPLC. For in-process-control, a sample of the reaction mixture, e.g. about 20-50 μL· volume, was diluted with about 2-10 mL of the diluent, if necessary, filtered with a syringe filter, and analyzed by HPLC.

The process of claim 4, further comprising the step of: (G) converting a free base of N-(5-ieri-butyl-isoxazol-3-yl)-N'-{4-[7-(2-morpholin-4-yl- ethoxy)imidazo[2,l-&][l,3]benzo-thiazol-2-yl]phenyl}urea to an acid addition salt of N- (5-ieri-butyl-isoxazol-3-yl)-N'-{4-[7-(2-morpholin-4-yl-ethoxy)imidazo[2,l- b] [ 1 ,3]benzo-thiazol-2-yl]phenyl } urea. 6. section.content: The invention relates to a preparation method of flumatinib and analogues thereof, a synthesis method of flumatinib and analogues thereof serving as a medicament for treating chronic myelogenous leukemia, and an intermediate obtained in the method. The preparation method has the advantages of easily available raw materials, simple operation and high product yield, and is suitable for industrial production.
本发明涉及一种氟马替尼及其类似物的制备方法，用于治疗慢性髓性白血病的药物氟马替尼及其类似物的合成方法，和包含在该方法中所获得的中间体。该制备方法原料易得、操作简单、产品收率高，适合工业化生产。

Preparation method of flumatinib and analogues thereof

Technical Field

The invention relates to the field of drug synthesis, and particularly relates to a preparation method of flumatinib mesylate and an analogue thereof.

Background

Chronic myeloid leukemia is a malignant proliferative leukemia disease of the myeloid lineage, and the leukemia cells still maintain differentiation ability, so there is a significant increase in granulocytes in each phase of bone marrow and blood, especially in the myeloblasts, the late granulocytes, the rod-shaped nuclei and the lobular granulocytes. The incidence rate of the chronic leukemia is about 20% of all leukemias and more than 90% of chronic leukemias according to domestic statistics, and is the most common chronic leukemia. The flumatinib and the analogue thereof are a new generation of chronic leukemia treatment drug, and the problem of drug resistance of Gleevec is well solved.

The structural formula of the flumatinib is as follows:

flumatinib is a small molecule Protein Tyrosine Kinase (PTK) inhibitor. By inhibiting the activity of Bcr-Abl tyrosine kinase, the tumor cell proliferation of Philadelphia chromosome positive CML and partial acute lymphocytic leukemia patients is inhibited, and the tumor cell apoptosis is induced. The preparation of the flumatinib has wide economic benefits and social effects.

The disclosed preparation methods of the intermediate of the flumatinib mainly comprise the following steps: patents CN107663151A, CN105008355B, CN105837575B and CN102548987B. The starting material used in the scheme has high price, and the reaction yield of the intermediate is low, so that the method is not beneficial to industrial production.

CN107663151A, CN105008355B and CN105837575B disclose a preparation method of flumatinib intermediate 4- [ (4-methyl-1-piperazinyl) methyl ] -3-trifluoromethylbenzoic acid, which comprises the following steps:

firstly protecting carboxyl, then carrying out substitution reaction with a bromization reagent, then carrying out reaction with N-methylpiperazine, and finally hydrolyzing to obtain a product. This method has the following problems: (1) the starting materials are not provided with suppliers in the market and need to be customized, and the customization process is expensive due to more steps and is not beneficial to commercial scale production; (2) the carboxyl needs to be protected and deprotected, the steps are complicated, and the yield is low; (3) the bromination step produces a lot of dibromo impurities inevitably, which affect the product quality of the intermediate.

CN102548987B discloses a preparation method of intermediate 4- [ (4-methyl-1-piperazinyl) methyl ] -3-trifluoromethylbenzoic acid:

firstly protecting carboxyl, then reacting with butyl lithium to introduce aldehyde group, then carrying out reductive amination reaction with N-methylpiperazine, and finally hydrolyzing to obtain the product. However, this method has the following problems: (1) the same problem exists in the initial raw materials, no supplier exists, and the commercial scale production is not facilitated; (2) butyl lithium and ester groups can generate side reaction in the step of introducing aldehyde groups, and the yield of products is seriously influenced while impurities are introduced.

Therefore, it is necessary to develop a synthetic method of flumatinib and its intermediate, which has simple operation, reasonable route and convenient commercial production (low price of starting materials and stable suppliers).

The application aims to invent a novel method for preparing flumatinib and analogues thereof.

Disclosure of Invention

The invention aims to solve the technical problems of industrialization and invents a novel method for preparing flumatinib and analogues thereof. The preparation method has the advantages of easily available raw materials, simple operation, high product yield and good selectivity, and is suitable for industrial production.

A compound of formula (IV):

r is selected from C 1 -C 6 Alkyl radical, C 1 -C 6 Haloalkyl, C 1 -C 6 Hydroxyalkyl radical, C 2 -C 6 Alkenyl, CO-R 1 、CO-OR 1 、SO 2 -R 1 Or SO-R 1 Is preferably C 1 -C 3 Alkyl radical, C 1 -C 3 Haloalkyl, C 1 -C 3 Hydroxyalkyl, more preferably methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, isopropyl, halomethyl, hydroxymethyl, and still more preferably methyl;

R 1 selected from hydrogen, C 1 -C 6 Alkyl radical, C 2 -C 6 Alkenyl, aryl or 3-to 7-membered heterocyclyl, preferably hydrogen, C 1 -C 3 Alkyl radical, C 2 -C 4 Alkenyl or phenyl.

A process for the preparation of a compound of formula (IV), comprising the steps of:

r is as defined in claim 1.

Preferably, the compound of formula (III) is reacted with an iodinating agent under strongly acidic conditions to give the compound of formula (IV); the strong acid is selected from concentrated sulfuric acid, acetic acid or trifluoroacetic acid, or a mixture of the strong acid, the acetic anhydride and the trifluoroacetic anhydride in any proportion; the iodine reagent is selected from one or more of iodine, N-iodosuccinimide, sodium periodate, sodium iodate, periodic acid and iodic acid.

Preferably, the mass ratio of the compound of formula (III) to the strong acid is 1:5 to 15, preferably 1:8 to 13, more preferably 1:10 to 12.

Preferably, the mass ratio of the compound of formula (III) to the iodinating agent is 1:1 to 5, preferably 1:1 to 3, more preferably 1:1 to 2.

The compound of formula (III) is prepared by reacting a compound of formula (II) with o-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde under the action of a reducing agent:

r is as defined in claim 1.

Preferably, the mass ratio of the compound of formula (II) to o-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde is 0.5 to 4:1, preferably 1 to 3:1, more preferably 1 to 2.5:1.

preferred reducing agents are selected from sodium borohydride, sodium triacetoxyborohydride, sodium cyanoborohydride, potassium borohydride, lithium aluminum hydride or lithium tri-sec-butylborohydride.

Preferably, the mass ratio of the compound of formula (II) to the reducing agent is 1:1 to 5, preferably 1:1 to 3, more preferably 1:1 to 2.

Preparation of a compound of formula (VII) and a compound of formula (VIII) to give a compound of formula (III):

wherein Y is a leaving group, preferably I, br, OTf, OTs or Oms.

Compounds of formula (VII) may be prepared by deprotecting a compound of formula (X):

wherein P is an amino protecting group, preferably Boc, cbz, fmoc, bn, bz or Ac.

Reacting a compound of formula (XI) with o-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde and a reducing agent to give a compound of formula (X):

p is an amino protecting group, preferably Boc, cbz, fmoc, bn, bz, ac.

A process for the preparation of a compound of formula (V), comprising the steps of:

preferably, the compound of formula (IV) is reacted with a metal organic reagent selected from one or more of n-butyl lithium, sec-butyl lithium, tert-butyl lithium, isopropyl magnesium chloride, isobutyl magnesium bromide, isopropyl magnesium chloride, sec-butyl magnesium bromide, methyl magnesium chloride to obtain the compound of formula (V), and carbon dioxide.

Preferably, the mass ratio of the compound of formula (IV) to the metal-organic reagent is 1:1 to 5, preferably 1:1 to 3, more preferably 1:1 to 2.

Reacting a compound of formula (V) with a compound of formula (VI) to produce a compound of formula (I):

r is as defined in claim 1.

On the other hand, the invention discloses a preparation method of flumatinib and the analogues thereof shown in formula I:

o-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde and a compound of a formula (II) are reacted under the action of a reaction solvent and a reducing agent to obtain a compound of a formula (III), and the reaction temperature is controlled to be 0-30 ℃; the reaction solvent is selected from tetrahydrofuran, dioxane, dichloromethane, 1, 2-dichloroethane, methanol, ethanol or a mixed solvent of any two in any proportion, preferably dichloromethane or tetrahydrofuran; the reducing agent is sodium borohydride, sodium triacetoxyborohydride, sodium cyanoborohydride, potassium borohydride, lithium aluminum hydride and lithium tri-sec-butylborohydride, and preferably sodium acetoxyborohydride or sodium cyanoborohydride; the amount of the reducing agent to be used is 1.0 to 5.0 equivalents, preferably 1.0 to 3.0 equivalents, and more preferably 1.0 to 2.5 equivalents.

Further, the compound of the formula (III) is subjected to iodination reaction to obtain a compound of a formula (IV), the selected solvent is concentrated sulfuric acid or the mixture of concentrated sulfuric acid and any one or two of three organic acids of acetic acid, acetic anhydride and trifluoroacetic acid in any proportion, and the dosage of the solvent is 3V-10V, preferably 3V-6V; the iodinating agent is selected from iodine, N-iodosuccinimide, sodium periodate, sodium iodate, periodic acid, iodic acid or a mixture of any two of them in any proportion, and the amount is 1.0 to 5.0 equivalents, preferably 1.0 to 3.0 equivalents, and more preferably 1.0 to 2.0 equivalents (calculated by iodine atom).

Further, after iodine-metal exchange is carried out on the compound of the formula (IV) and an active metal reagent, the compound of the formula (V) is obtained by reaction with carbon dioxide, wherein the selected active metal reagent is selected from n-butyl lithium, sec-butyl lithium, tert-butyl lithium, isopropyl magnesium chloride, isobutyl magnesium bromide, isopropyl magnesium chloride, sec-butyl magnesium bromide, methyl magnesium chloride or a mixture of any two of the above in any proportion, preferably n-butyl lithium, isopropyl magnesium chloride or a mixture of the above, and the dosage is 1.0 to 5.0 equivalents, preferably 1.0 to 3.0 equivalents, and more preferably 1.0 to 2.0 equivalents; the reaction solvent is selected from 1, 4-dioxane, 2-methyltetrahydrofuran, tetrahydrofuran, toluene or their mixture, and the amount is 4-10V, preferably 4-8V.

Further, the compound of formula (V) and the compound of formula (VI) react under the action of a condensing agent to obtain the compound of formula (I), wherein the selected condensing agent is selected from 1-ethyl- (3-dimethylaminopropyl) carbodiimide hydrochloride, dicyclohexylcarbodiimide, diisopropylcarbodiimide, N, N, N ', N' -tetramethyl-O- (7-azabenzotriazol-1-yl) urea hexafluorophosphate, O- (benzotriazol-1-yl) -N, N, N ', N' -tetramethylurea hexafluorophosphate, O-benzotriazol-N, N, N-tetramethylurea tetrafluoroborate or a mixture of any one of the compounds and an activating agent 1-hydroxybenzotriazole or 4-dimethylaminopyridine in any proportion, and the using amount is 0.5-5.0 equivalent; the selected solvent is water, pyridine, N-dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, 1, 4-dioxane, dimethyl sulfoxide or a mixed solvent of any two of the water, the pyridine, the N, N-dimethylformamide, the tetrahydrofuran, the 1, 4-dioxane and the dimethyl sulfoxide in any proportion, and the dosage is 4V-10V.

Detailed Description

Embodiments of the present invention will be described in detail below with reference to specific examples. The following examples are merely illustrative of the present invention and should not be construed as limiting the scope of the invention.

Example 1

Synthesis of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethylbenzyl) piperazine (III-1)

20kg of o-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde and 17.25kg of N-methylpiperazine were added to 60L of methylene chloride, and the mixture was stirred for half an hour to obtain a reaction solution (1). Adding 24.34Kg of sodium triacetoxyborohydride (reducing agent) and 10L of acetic acid into the reaction solution (1), dropwise adding the mixture to react for 6 to 8 hours, pouring 180L of aqueous solution into the reaction system, adjusting the pH to 9 to 10, adding 200L of dichloromethane to extract an aqueous phase, separating the aqueous phase, drying the aqueous phase with anhydrous sodium sulfate, and concentrating an organic phase until the organic phase is dried to obtain 29Kg of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethylbenzyl) piperazine. The molar yield was 98%.

Example 2

Synthesis of 1-benzoyl-4- (2-trifluoromethylbenzyl) piperazine (X-1)

To 60mL of methylene chloride were added 20g of o-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde and 32.76g of N-benzoylpiperazine, and the mixture was stirred for half an hour to obtain a reaction mixture (1). Adding 21.87g of sodium triacetoxyborohydride and 11mL of acetic acid into the reaction solution (1), after dropwise addition reaction for 5-6 hours, pouring 160mL of aqueous solution into the reaction system, adjusting the pH value to 9-10, adding 200mL of dichloromethane to extract an aqueous phase, separating the aqueous phase, drying the aqueous phase by using anhydrous sodium sulfate, and concentrating an organic phase until the organic phase is dried to obtain 37.6g of 1-benzoyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl benzyl) piperazine. The molar yield was 94%.

Example 3

Synthesis of 1- (9-fluorenylmethoxycarbonyl) -4- (2-trifluoromethylbenzyl) piperazine (X-2)

2kg of o-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde and 4.25kg of N-Fmoc-piperazine were added to 6L of methylene chloride, and the mixture was stirred for half an hour to obtain a reaction solution (1). Adding 2.19Kg of sodium triacetoxyborohydride and 1.4L of acetic acid into the reaction solution (1), after dropwise adding reaction for 7-8 hours, pouring 19L of aqueous solution into the reaction system, adjusting the pH value to 9-10, adding 20L of dichloromethane to extract a water phase, separating liquid, drying by using anhydrous sodium sulfate, and concentrating an organic phase to dryness to obtain 4.82Kg1- (9-fluorenylmethoxycarbonyl) -4- (2-trifluoromethylbenzyl) piperazine with the molar yield of 90%.

Example 4

Synthesis of 1- (tert-butoxycarbonyl) -4- (2-trifluoromethylbenzyl) piperazine (X-3)

To 60mL of methylene chloride were added 20g of o-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde and 32.07g of N-Boc-piperazine, followed by stirring for half an hour to obtain a reaction mixture (1). Adding 21.87g of sodium triacetoxyborohydride and 14mL of acetic acid into the reaction solution (1), dropwise adding the mixture to react for 7-8 hours, pouring 190mL of aqueous solution into the reaction system, adjusting the pH value to 9-10, adding 200mL of dichloromethane to extract an aqueous phase, separating liquid, drying the aqueous phase by using anhydrous sodium sulfate, and concentrating an organic phase until the organic phase is dried to obtain 36g of 1- (tert-butyloxycarbonyl) -4- (2-trifluoromethylbenzyl) piperazine with the molar yield of 91%.

Example 5

Synthesis of 4- (2-trifluoromethyl-benzyl) piperazine (VII)

To 60mL of methylene chloride were added 20g of o-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde and 12g of piperazine, followed by stirring for half an hour to obtain a reaction mixture (1). Adding 21.87g of sodium triacetoxyborohydride and 14mL of acetic acid into the reaction solution (1), dropwise adding the mixture to react for 7-8 hours, pouring 190mL of aqueous solution into the reaction system, adjusting the pH value to 9-10, adding 200mL of dichloromethane to extract an aqueous phase, separating the solution, drying the solution by using anhydrous sodium sulfate, and concentrating an organic phase until the organic phase is dried to obtain 25.8g of 4- (2-trifluoromethyl-benzyl) piperazine with the molar yield of 92%.

Example 6

Synthesis of 4- (2-trifluoromethyl-benzyl) piperazine (VII)

After 2.33kg of 1-fluorenylmethoxycarbonyl-4- (2-trifluoromethylbenzyl) piperazine was dissolved in 17L of dichloromethane, 500g of ammonia was added and reacted at 25 ℃ for 10 hours, 10L of water was added to separate the solution, the organic phase was washed twice with water (5L each), dried over anhydrous sodium sulfate and concentrated to dryness to obtain 1.10kg of 4- (2-trifluoromethyl-benzyl) piperazine with a molar yield of 90%.

Example 7

Synthesis of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-benzyl) piperazine (III-1)

Dissolving 24.43g of 4- (2-trifluoromethyl-benzyl) piperazine in 200mL of THF, stirring for dissolving, cooling to 0-5 ℃, slowly adding 4g of sodium hydride, stirring at 0-5 ℃ for 1.5-2.0 h, adding 14g of methyl iodide, reacting at 25 ℃ for 10h, adding 30mL of water for quenching, adjusting the pH value to 12-13 by using 10% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, adding 100mL of ethyl acetate, separating, collecting an organic phase, drying by using anhydrous sodium sulfate, concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain 20.66g of 4- (2-trifluoromethyl-benzyl) piperazine, and obtaining the product with the molar yield of 80%.

Example 8

Synthesis of 1-ethyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-benzyl) piperazine (III-2)

Dissolving 24.43g of 4- (2-trifluoromethyl-benzyl) piperazine in 200mL of THF, stirring for dissolving, cooling to 0-5 ℃, slowly adding 4g of sodium hydride, stirring at 0-5 ℃ for 1.5-2.0 h, adding 15.4g of iodoethane, reacting at 25 ℃ for 10h, adding 30mL of water for quenching, adjusting the pH value to 12-13 by using 10% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, adding 100mL of ethyl acetate, separating, collecting an organic phase, drying by using anhydrous sodium sulfate, concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain 21.52g of 4- (2-trifluoromethyl-benzyl) piperazine, and obtaining the product with the molar yield of 79%.

Example 9

Synthesis of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-4-iodo-benzyl) piperazine (IV)

170L of concentrated sulfuric acid is added into a reaction kettle respectively, 29kg of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl benzyl) piperazine and 15kg of iodine are added slowly, 14.5kg of sodium periodate is added slowly after stirring for 0.5 hour, and the reaction is carried out for 6 to 8 hours at the temperature of between 30 and 40 ℃. Slowly adding the reaction solution into 200L of ice water, and quenching the reaction; the pH was adjusted to 11 to 12 with sodium hydroxide solution, and the aqueous layer was extracted with 300L of dichloromethane and separated. The organic phase is decompressed and concentrated to be dry, and 39Kg of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-4-iodo-benzyl) piperazine is obtained with the molar yield of 90 percent.

1 H NMR(400MHz,DMSO-d 6 ):δ7.99(d,J＝8.0Hz,1H),7.94(s,1H),7.52(d,J＝8.0Hz,1H),3.52(s,2H),2.36-2.29(m,8H),2.14(s,3H)。

MS(ESI)m/z＝385[M+H] + 。

Example 10

Synthesis of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-4-iodo-benzyl) piperazine (IV)

170L of concentrated sulfuric acid is respectively added into a reaction kettle, 29kg of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl benzyl) piperazine and 30kg of iodine are slowly added, and the reaction is carried out for 6 to 8 hours at the temperature of between 30 and 40 ℃. Slowly adding the reaction solution into 200L of ice water, and quenching the reaction; the pH was adjusted to 11 to 12 with sodium hydroxide solution, and the aqueous layer was extracted with 300L of dichloromethane and separated. The organic phase is decompressed and concentrated to be dry, and 30Kg of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-4-iodo-benzyl) piperazine is obtained with the molar yield of 70%.

Example 11

Synthesis of 4- (4-methylpiperazin-1-ylmethyl) benzoic acid (V)

20kg of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-4-iodo-benzyl) piperazine (1.0 eq) was added to a reaction kettle, 200L of tetrahydrofuran was added, after nitrogen substitution, temperature was controlled at-85 ℃ to-75 ℃, 27L (1.3 eq) of a 2.5M n-butyl lithium n-hexane solution was added dropwise, and the reaction was completed for 3 to 4 hours. With CO 2 Gas is introduced into the gas replacement system once and then continuously introduced with CO 2 And reacting for 2-3 hours. Adding 150L methanol, quenching, adding 12Kg concentrated sulfuric acid, stirring for 0.5 hr, and vacuum filtering to obtain 26.6Kg of compoundThe quantitative yield was 133%.

20g of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-4-iodo-benzyl) piperazine (1.0 eq) is added into a reaction kettle, 200mL of tetrahydrofuran is added, after nitrogen replacement, the temperature is controlled to be-85 to-75 ℃, 41.5mL (2.0 eq) of 2.5M n-butyl lithium n-hexane solution is added dropwise, and the reaction is finished for 3 to 4 hours. 20g of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-4-iodo-benzyl) piperazine (1.0 eq) was added to a reaction kettle, 200mL of 2-methyltetrahydrofuran was added, nitrogen was replaced, the temperature was controlled to-85 ℃ to-75 ℃, 27mL (1.3 eq) of a 2.5M n-butyllithium n-hexane solution was added dropwise, and the reaction was completed for 3 to 4 hours. Then adding 150mL of methanol for quenching reaction, dropwise adding 12g of concentrated sulfuric acid, stirring for 0.5 hour after dropwise adding, and performing suction filtration to obtain 26.9g of the compound shown in the formula (V) with the mass yield of 134%.

Example 14

Synthesis of 4- (4-methylpiperazin-1-ylmethyl) benzoic acid (V)

Adding 20g of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-4-iodine-benzyl) piperazine into a reaction kettle, adding 200mL of tetrahydrofuran, replacing nitrogen, controlling the temperature to be-45 to-55 ℃, dropwise adding 62mL of 1M tetrahydrofuran solution of isopropyl magnesium chloride and lithium chloride, and reacting for 3-4 hours after dropwise adding. With CO 2 Gas is introduced into the gas replacement system once and then continuously introduced with CO 2 And reacting for 2-3 hours. The reaction was quenched by addition of 150mL of methanol12g of concentrated sulfuric acid is dripped, stirred for 0.5 hour after dripping, and then the compound of the formula (V) 24.3g is obtained by suction filtration, with the mass yield of 122%.

Example 15

Synthesis of 4- (4-methylpiperazin-1-ylmethyl) benzoic acid (V)

Adding 20g of 1-methyl-4- (2-trifluoromethyl-4-iodo-benzyl) piperazine into a reaction kettle, adding 200mL of tetrahydrofuran, replacing nitrogen, controlling the temperature to be-35 to-45 ℃, dropwise adding 26mL of 2M tetrahydrofuran solution of isopropyl magnesium chloride, dropwise adding 27mL of 2.5M n-butyl lithium n-hexane solution after dropwise adding is finished, and carrying out heat preservation reaction for 3-4 hours after dropwise adding is finished. With CO 2 The gas in the gas replacement system is continuously introduced with CO again 2 And reacting for 2-3 hours. Then adding 150mL of methanol to quench the reaction, dropwise adding 12g of concentrated sulfuric acid, stirring for 0.5 hour after the dropwise adding, and performing suction filtration to obtain 24.8g of the compound shown in the formula (V) with the mass yield of 124%.

Example 16

Synthesis of flumatinib (I)

50g of N- (2-methyl-5-aminopyridin-3-yl) -4- (pyridin-3-yl) pyrimidine-2-amine, 100g of 4- (4-methylpiperazin-1-ylmethyl) benzoic acid and 103g of EDC are dissolved in 250mL of pyridine, stirred at room temperature for 3-4 hours, after the reaction is finished, 125mL of ammonia water is added, stirred and crystallized for 2-3 hours, and then the mixture is filtered by suction, and the solid is dried to obtain 91g of flumatinib with the molar yield of 90%.

section.content: 本发明提供了包含种或多种化合物的组合物，所述化合物的结构包含：具有4至12个碳原子的节点结构、个或多个(聚)甘油基基团、以及个或多个疏水部分，其中所述个或多个(聚)甘油基基团的每个都通过第初级连接基连接到所述节点结构上，所述个或多个疏水部分各自独立地通过初级连接基连接到所述节点结构上或通过次级连接基连接到所述(聚)甘油基基团之上，并且其中所述聚甘油基增稠剂的平均甘油基聚合度大于3至小于约11，以及每个初级连接基的平均疏水基团数为约0.35或更大。本发明还提供了聚甘油基化合物，包含水、表面活性剂和聚甘油基增稠剂的组合物，以及制备本发明的聚甘油基化合物和组合物的方法。

The invention provides the compositions comprising one or more compounds, the structure of described compound comprises: have the node structure of 4 to 12 carbon atoms, one or more (gathering) glyceryl group, and one or more hydrophobic part, each of wherein said one or more (gathering) glyceryl group is connected on described node structure by the first primary linker, the one or more hydrophobic part is connected on described node structure by primary linker independently of one another or is connected on one of described (gathering) glyceryl group by secondary linker, and the average glyceryl degree of polymerization of wherein said polyglyceryl thickening agent is more than 3 to less than about 11, and the average hydrophobicity group number of each primary linker is about 0.35 or bigger.Present invention also offers Polyglyceryl compounds, comprise water, surfactant and the compositions of polyglyceryl thickening agent, and prepare Polyglyceryl compounds and the method for compositions of the present invention.

Polyglyceryl compounds and compositionsThe cross reference of related application

The temporary patent application No.61/413,712's that patent application claims was submitted on November 15th, 2010 is preferential Power, this full patent texts is incorporated by reference herein.

Technical field

The present invention relates to can be used for the polyglyceryl thickening agent of multiple application (including human body clean) and comprise polyglycereol The compositions of base thickening agent.

Background technology

Synthectic detergents, such as cationic surfactant, anion surfactant, amphoteric surfactant and non-from Sub-surface activating agent, is widely used in multiple detergent and Cleasing compositions, to give its clean-up performance.Additionally, at some In compositions (such as personal care composition, such as shampoo, abluent etc.), may be desirable for surfactant and such as The one-tenth split-phase mixing of thickening agent, relatively to realize such as gentleness degree, foam volume, foam stability and rheological behaviour etc property The required balance of energy.

Conventional thickening agent kind (often comprises more than 100 for having bigger polar head group and height ethoxylation Mole oxirane (EO)) amphiphile, amphiphilic molecule.Regrettably, ethoxylation needs the EO of large volume, and this is that one passes through second The air oxidation of alkene gas and the gaseous petroleum chemical industry derivant that synthesizes.EO has obvious health and safety risk, is a kind of Intractable chemicals, in addition, owing to crude oil and gas reserves are limited, in the long run, many people are regarded as Unsustainable product.Additionally, the by-product of ethoxylation is cyclic ethers Isosorbide-5-Nitrae-twoAlkane, it is under high exposure level can Doubt carcinogen.Ethoxylation material usually contains the Isosorbide-5-Nitrae-two of trace (10-100ppm)Alkane, it is necessary to use special separation side Method (such as vacuum extraction) could be by Isosorbide-5-Nitrae-twoThe content of alkane drops to undetectable level.Isosorbide-5-Nitrae-twoAlkane is with trace level When being present in Cleasing compositions, it is not intended as that necessarily there is health or security risk.But, with Isosorbide-5-Nitrae-twoIt is negative that alkane is correlated with Public image has promoted people to look for the product without ethoxylation material.

Inventor has recognized that, it is desirable to the ethoxylation thickening agent of more natural and reproducible material substitution synthesis.So And, it is special that the natural thickener of such as plant gum is generally of the most worthless viscosity, pseudoplastic behavior and/or elastic rheology Property.Therefore, it was recognized by the inventor that be highly desirable to prepare the compositions with more natural and/or reproducible thickening agent, to subtract Few disadvantages mentioned above.Inventor it is also recognized that, it is not necessary to the thickening agent of ethoxylation is highly desirable, especially can be with aesthetics On the thickening agent of a series of personal care product formulations of mode thickening that can be esthetically acceptable to the consumers.

Summary of the invention

The invention provides polyglyceryl thickening agent, which overcome the shortcoming of prior art and interpolation can be improved this gathers The viscosity of the compositions of glyceryl thickening agent.

According to an aspect, the invention provides the polyglyceryl compositions comprising one or more compounds, describedization The structure of compound comprises: have the node structure of 4 to 12 carbon atoms, one or more (gathering) glyceryl group and one or Multiple hydrophobic parts, each of wherein said one or more (gathering) glyceryl group is connected by the first primary linker On described node structure, the one or more hydrophobic part is connected to described node by primary linker independently of one another It is connected on one of described (gathering) glyceryl group in structure or by secondary linker, and wherein polyglyceryl thickening agent The average glyceryl degree of polymerization be more than 3 to about 11, and the average hydrophobicity group number of each primary linker is about 0.35 or more Greatly.

For the sake of clarity, carry for the polyglyceryl thickening agent (E1A) comprising polyglyceryl methyl dioleate Supply following exemplary calculating.Obtain methyl dioleate as stated above, Natrulon H-10 (derives from Lonza Group's Natrulon H-10) and polyglyceryl methyl dioleate polyglyceryl thickening agent (E1A)1H H NMR spectroscopy.According to first Base glucose dioleate and the reference spectra of Natrulon H-10, polyglyceryl thickening agent proton peak between 3-4ppm in spectrum Belong to five protons of polyglyceryl repetitive and five overlapping proton of methyl dioleate, 1-1.3ppm it Between proton peak belong to 40 diagnostic protons (see, e.g. Fig. 3 and 4) on methyl dioleate hydrophobic group.Meter Calculate the area under curve at peak, then use below equation to calculate DPg:

Hydrophilic index is tested:

Use the Antaris FT-NIR analyser (Thermo being furnished with transmission, optical fiber and integrating sphere diffuse-reflectance module Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA) various polyglyceryl thickening agents are carried out hydrophilic index test.Use and immerse Minor diameter (1/8 inch) transmittance probes (AxiomAnalytical, Inc, Tustin, CA) in glass reactor carries out in real time, Online optical fiber measurement.Use integrating sphere module to carry out off-line measurement, and use Thermo Fisher Scientific to provide TQ Analyst software program analytical data.The spectrum of online and off-line measurement is all with 4cm-1Resolution scan 64 times obtain.

The NIR spectra that a series of use integrating sphere modules carry out off-line, indoor temperature measurement acquisition carries out polyglyceryl increasing Hydrophilic index (HI) test of thick dose.HI is that hydrophilic (glyceryl unit) feature of prepared polyglyceryl thickening agent is with hydrophobic (hydrocarbon) feature is proportional amount of to be measured.Use TQAnalyst the software ,-OH absorption band (6,100cm to glyceryl unit-1Extremely 7500cm-1Under), area is integrated, then with hydrophobic hydrocarbon SPECTRAL REGION (5320cm-1To 6050cm-1Integral area under) enters Row compares.Calculating ratio between hydrophilic and hydrophobic area is HI, and can be used for the hydrophilic between the different sample of comparison.HI Ratio is unrelated with sample size/thickness and test temperature.Relatively low hydrophilic index value shows to be mixed with less polyglycereol list Unit.Similarly, higher hydrophilic index will indicate that more polyglycereol is mixed with polyglyceryl thickening agent.Preferred at some In embodiment, HI is about 0.3 or bigger.Preferred HI is about 0.4 or bigger.In certain embodiments, preferred HI is about 0.4 to about 0.9, and preferred HI is about 0.5 to about 0.8.

Zero-shear viscosity is tested:

On various personal care compositions, following zero-shear viscosity test is carried out to measure viscosity according to the present invention.25 Proof stress flow graph (AR-2000, TA Instruments Ltd., NewCastle, DE, USA) is used to test at DEG C The viscosity measurement of formula.Double-walled Couette geometry is used to perform steady state shearing stress scans at 25.0 ± 0.1 DEG C.By Rheology Advantage software v4.1.10 (TAInstruments Ltd., New Castle, DE, USA) performs data acquisition Collection and analysis.For showing the sample of Newtonian behavior, its zero shearing apparent viscosity is with (0.005-in the range of shear stress Average viscosity value 100Pa) obtained is reported.For pseudoplastic behavior (shear thinning) fluid, zero shears apparent viscosity (η 0) leads to Cross the shear stress scan data the Fitting Calculation to Ellis Viscosity Model.

Formula foam is tested:

According to the present invention, various Cleasing compositions are carried out formula as below foam test, to measure foams during stirring Long-pending.First, the solution of preparation test compositions in Simulative tap water.In order to embody hardness of water from the beginning, by the chlorination of 0.36g Calcium is dissolved in 995g deionized water.Then five (5.0) grams of test compositionss are added in this solution, and mix until uniformly. In order to determine formula foam volume, test compositions (1000mL) is added to SITA R-2000 foam tester (can be from Future DigitalScientific, Co. (Bethpage, N.Y.) are commercially available) sample cell in.Test parameter is configured with Process (sequence number=3) in triplicate, sample size is 250ml (addition=250ml), 15 seconds mixings time of each cycle Being 13 mixing cycles (stirred for several=13) in (mixing time=15 second), rotor is with the revolution (revolution=1200) of 1200RPM Rotating, temperature is set to 30 DEG C ± 2 DEG C.At the end of each mixing cycle, gather foam volume data, and determine three processes Meansigma methods and standard deviation.Maximum foam volume is reported as the value after the 13rd mixing cycle.

Example 1: the preparation (E1-E17) of polyglyceryl thickening agent

Prepare polyglyceryl thickener blend composition E1 as follows: to suitable size be furnished with nitrogen bubbler, reflux condenser, Graduated charging hopper and NIR reaction probe container in add 0.058 moles glucose dioleate (MGD), 0.050 mole of carbonic acid glyceride and 0.0058 mole of Ca (OCH3)2.Heat the mixture to about 80 DEG C, be subsequently placed in vacuum Under, make mixture deaerate.Breaking vacuum, advertises mixture at nitrogen and is heated slowly to 190 DEG C under agitation as appropriate.190 After balancing 30 minutes at DEG C, in 2.5 hours, GC (degassed process, 1.15 moles) is slowly added in reactor.Complete to add After adding, at 190 DEG C, stir reactant mixture, until carbonic acid glyceride consumes completely.Analyzed by real-time NIR and monitor institute on the spot De-airing step (moisture measurement), reaction process and GC is had to consume.After completing reaction, being cooled down by material, it is suitable to enter In container.

By changing the type of following raw material and the polyglyceryl thickening agent E2-E17 that ratio synthesis is additional: polymerization causes Agent, glyceryl monomer and/or base catalyst.The change of raw materials used, reaction condition and product collects in table 1 below.For E9- E11 and E13, will be sequentially added in reactant mixture according to AGC and GC of operation synthesis hereafter, and for E14, then will AGC and GC is simultaneously introduced.It addition, feed time, the total reaction time of various reaction and temperature and gained in table 2 below Illustrate.Which use following abbreviation: MGD=methyl dioleate, SO=sorbitan oleate, SSO=is dehydrated Sorbitol sesquioleate, GC=carbonic acid glyceride, AGC/GC=acetylation carbonic acid glyceride and the combination of carbonic acid glyceride, SDO=sorbitan dioleate.

The preparation of acetyl group carbonic acid glyceride (AGC): the following is the general laboratory scale operation preparing AGC: Xiang Gan Only, the 250mL two neck flask being furnished with magnetic stirring apparatus, near-infrared probe and condenser being dried adds carbonic acid glyceride (82.6g, 0.70mol), acetic anhydride (70.0g, 0.68mol) and two pyridines.In the first stage of reaction, 100 DEG C return Flow down and content is heated 6 hours.By following carbonic acid glyceride at 7000cm-1Feature-OH (hydroxyl) absorption band at place disappears, The carbonic acid glyceride conversion to AcGC is monitored by FT-NIR.Reaction second stage, 45 DEG C and decompression (end vacuum= 3.5 torr) under be removed by distillation acetic acid by-product.By following at 6850cm-1The disappearance at acetic acid peak, place, is monitored second by near-infrared The removing situation of acid.The AcGC of gained is stored under nitrogen envelope, until using.

section.content: The new amino-acid modified silane compound with faintly acid amino and alkalescence carboxyl can be used as surface conditioning agent or fabric-treating agent.
具有弱酸性氨基和弱碱性羧基的新的氨基酸改性的硅烷化合物可用作表面处理剂或织物处理剂。

Amino-acid modified silane compound and preparation methodTechnical field

The present invention relates to new amino-acid modified silane compound and preparation method thereof.These silane compounds Can be used as oil paint additive, binding agent, silane coupler, fabric-treating agent, surface conditioning agent etc..

Background technology

Can be used as being applied over base material and give the surface of its surface hydrophilicity containing amino silane compounds is known and process Agent, and it is used as to be applied over fiber and fabric gives the fabric-treating agent of its hydrophilicity.Exemplary compounds Including TSL 8330, amino propyl methyl dimethoxysilane, aminoethylaminopropyl Trimethoxy silane and aminoethylaminopropyl methyl dimethoxysilane.Although these contain Aminosilylation Compound can give hydrophilicity, but they surface soilings that imparting was processed and anti-fungal property side Face validity is the best.

Those can not only give hydrophilic, and can give the surface soiling and anti-fungal property processed Compound be also known.Such as, patent document 1 discloses that the silane compound with tetraalkyl ammonium, Such as 3-(trimethoxysilyl) propyl group octadecyldimethyl ammonium chloride.Patent documentation 1 and 2 disclosure Comprise the silane compound of thetine group, and comprise the silane compound of quaternary ammonium and carboxyl.

But, these compounds are the most unsatisfactory.Although described in patent documentation 1 containing tetra-allkylammonium Silane compound can give anti-fungal property, but they can not give gratifying antifouling property, Because the base material processed or fabric face be static electrification positive charge due to tetraalkylammonium group so that dust and Chip deposits from the teeth outwards due to Electrostatic Absorption.Because the sulfur-bearing described in patent documentation 2 and 3 is for Radix Betae The silane compound containing tetra-allkylammonium and carboxyl described in the silane compound of alkali and patent documentation 3 is at molecule Middle formation amphion, so compared with processing with the silane compound containing tetra-allkylammonium, the base material processed Or the less electrostatic charge of fabric face band.Silane chemical combination owing to sulfonic group is strong acid, containing thetine Thing has the bias towards negative charge side, and due to tetraalkylammonium group, containing tetra-allkylammonium and the silicon of carboxyl Hydride compounds has the bias towards positive charge side.Due to relative to electroneutral this bias, it is impossible to obtain Gratifying antifouling property.

Quotation list

Patent documentation 1:JP-A S63-500991 (WO 87/06470)

Patent documentation 2:JP-A H05-222064

Patent documentation 3:WO 1995/10523 (USP 5936703)

Summary of the invention

It is an object of the present invention to provide and can give the most antifouling and hydrophilicity amino-acid modified silicon Hydride compounds and preparation method thereof.

Inventor has been found that hydrophilic and the soil resistance of certain amino-acid modified silane compound display improvement Can, because it does not has electric charge to bias.When this compound is used as surface conditioning agent or fabric-treating agent, process The surface crossed keeps electric neutrality, because electric charge is offset by faintly acid amino and alkalescence carboxyl.Therefore, by electricity The surface adsorption that lotus causes is minimized.Which ensure that hydrophilic and the antifouling property of improvement.

On the one hand, the present invention provides one to have an amino-acid modified silane compound of formula (1):

Wherein R1、R4And R5It is respectively hydrogen or substituted or unsubstituted C1-C20Monovalent hydrocarbon, R2And R3It is respectively Substituted or unsubstituted C1-C20Monovalent hydrocarbon, R6For hydrogen, substituted or unsubstituted C1-C20Monovalent hydrocarbon or carboxylic Base, a is 0 or 1, and b is the integer of 1 to 10, and n is 0,1 or 2.

Preferably in formula (1), R1、R4And R5It is respectively hydrogen, R6For hydrogen or carboxyl, a is 1, and b is 3, and And n is 0,1 or 2.

The silane compound of typical formula (1) is N-(3-trimethoxy-silylpropyl)-Beta-alanine, N-(3-methyl dimethoxy epoxide silylpropyl)-Beta-alanine, N-(3-triethoxysilylpropyltetrasulfide)- Beta-alanine, N-(3-methyl diethoxy silylpropyl)-Beta-alanine, N-(3-trimethoxy first Ethoxysilylpropyl) aspartic acid, N-(3-methyl dimethoxy epoxide silylpropyl) aspartic acid, N-(3-tri- Triethoxysilyl propyl group) aspartic acid, and N-(3-methyl diethoxy silylpropyl) Radix Asparagi ammonia Acid.

On the other hand, the present invention provides the method for the amino-acid modified silane compound of a kind of formula (1), De-three organic group first including the amino-acid modified silane compound that the silicyl with formula (2) is protected Silicon alkyl reaction:

Wherein R1' for R1Or three organosilyl groups R10R11R12Si-, R1、R4And R5Be respectively hydrogen or replacement or Unsubstituted C1-C20Monovalent hydrocarbon, R2And R3It is respectively substituted or unsubstituted C1-C20Monovalent hydrocarbon, R6' it is Hydrogen, substituted or unsubstituted C1-C20Monovalent hydrocarbon or the carboxyl of silicyl protection, R7、R8、R9、R10、R11 And R12It is respectively substituted or unsubstituted C1-C20Monovalent hydrocarbon, a is 0 or 1, and b is the integer of 1 to 10, And n is 0,1 or 2.

The beneficial effect of the invention

When this amino-acid modified silane compound is used as surface conditioning agent or fabric-treating agent, processed Surface keeps electric neutrality, because electric charge is offset by faintly acid amino and alkalescence carboxyl.Therefore, electric charge draw The surface adsorption risen is minimized.Which ensure that hydrophilic and the antifouling property of improvement.Therefore this compound may be used As oil paint additive, binding agent, silane coupler, fabric-treating agent and surface conditioning agent etc..

Detailed description of the invention

Symbol used herein (Cn-Cm) represents the group comprising n to m carbon atom in each group.

One embodiment of the invention is to have the amino-acid modified silane compound of formula (1):

Work as R1、R4、R5And R6For optionally substituted C1-C20During monovalent hydrocarbon, suitable alkyl include straight chain, Branched or cyclic alkyl, thiazolinyl, aryl and aralkyl.Particularly Exemplary groups includes straight chained alkyl, example As methyl, ethyl, propyl group, butyl, amyl group, hexyl, heptyl, octyl group, decyl, dodecyl, ten Tetraalkyl, cetyl, octadecyl and eicosyl；Branched-alkyl, such as isopropyl, isobutyl group, The tert-butyl group, tertiary hexyl (thexyl) and 2-ethylhexyl；Cyclic alkyl, such as cyclopenta and cyclohexyl；Alkene Base, such as vinyl, pi-allyl and acrylic；Aryl, such as phenyl and tolyl；And aralkyl, Such as benzyl.Especially, methyl and ethyl are preferred.Some or all of hydrogen atoms on alkyl are permissible Being replaced, the most suitably substituent group includes alkoxyl, such as methoxyl group, ethyoxyl and (different) propoxyl group； Halogen atom, such as fluorine, chlorine, bromine and iodine；Cyano group；Amino；C6-C18Aryl, such as phenyl and tolyl； C7-C18Aralkyl, such as benzyl and phenethyl；Ester group；Ether；Acyl group；Thioether group；Alkylsilyl groups Base, and alkoxysilyl, and combinations of the above.
